trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Poetry, "do-gooders" and using AI for good
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 11/10/25

urls.page_title: Summoning religion, mythology and memory ‘Within the Shop of the Divine’
urls.page_summary: A Saint Anthony statue that glows in the dark lights the way into poems that connect people beyond death, visit holy sites, consider Satanic bargains and consult astrology.
urls.page_title: The do-gooder dilemma: to disclose or not to disclose | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: People say they would feel worse telling others about their charitable acts than if they kept the news to themselves, or told others about their personal achievements, the study found.
urls.page_title: Faculty win Bezos grants to use AI for environmental challenges | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Cornell has won three of 15 major grants from the Bezos Earth Fund, awarded to leverage artificial intelligence in the fight against climate change and environmental challenges.
urls.page_title: Collaborative Pathways for AI Funding Panel (November 2025) | Cornell Research Services
urls.page_summary: Explore how collaborative partnerships drive AI research innovation and funding success. Engage with Cornell faculty who have successfully built impactful collaborations and learn about institutional resources, including the Cornell AI Initiative.

23 likes, 0 comments - cornellcurb on October 20, 2025: "Get excited for CURBx Fall 2025!!! We have some amazing undergraduate researchers coming to discuss their projects with us. We hope to see you there!".
urls.page_title: Event considers political lessons learned from COVID
urls.page_summary: A Nov. 13 event sponsored by the College of Arts & Sciences will feature reflections on the political and social context and consequences of the Covid epidemic.
urls.page_title: Office of National Fellowships | Office of National Fellowships
The College of Arts & Sciences, Cornell University
urls.page_summary: Liberal arts college at Cornell University. A home for exploration, discovery and imagination. Undergraduate, faculty excellence in research and education. Academic diversity in the humanities, biological sciences and discipline-based education.
urls.page_title: Indonesian women created a political voice through gendered care work
urls.page_summary: In "Domestic Nationalism," Chiara Formichi argues that during the 1920s to 1950s, Indonesian women’s domestic activities contributed to nation-building as a political project.
